ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product entry; no exact entry for braised skinless chicken wing. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKN covers cooked chicken wing; no sub-code for braised without skin. Closest parent match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for braised skinless chicken wing; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately one braised skinless chicken wing with bone, weighing around 45 g edible portion.

nutrient_source.title: Braised chicken wing, without skin — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for braised chicken wing without skin (F11010021). All core macros and micronutrients sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: N/A — Negligible carbohydrate food; GI not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Braised chicken wing without skin contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI testing is performed on zero-carbohydrate protein foods. Glycemic index field is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value indicating not-applicable for a negligible-carbohydrate food; gi_category is set to not_applicable. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium at 73 mg/100 g with modest potassium at 153 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Braised chicken wing without skin is low in sodium (73 mg/100 g) and suitable for hypertension management; however, individuals should request minimal soy sauce in the braising liquid, as commercial preparations can substantially increase sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar with high protein (27.18 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, this food has negligible glycaemic impact and is an excellent high-protein choice for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes; individuals should verify that braising sauces do not contain added sugar or starchy thickeners.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate cholesterol (74 mg/100 g) and estimated saturated fat (~2.0 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Braised skinless chicken wing is relatively lean with low saturated fat (~2.0 g/100 g), but the moderate cholesterol content (74 mg/100 g) warrants portion awareness for individuals with established cardiovascular disease; removing the skin significantly reduces fat and cholesterol compared to skin-on preparations.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of omega-3 fatty acids and dietary fibre; no meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Braised skinless chicken wing provides low saturated fat and no trans fat, but lacks omega-3 fatty acids and dietary fibre required for anti-inflammatory benefit; pair with fatty fish, leafy greens, or ginger-turmeric dishes to improve the overall anti-inflammatory profile of the meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Braised chicken wing without skin (HPB Singapore food code F11010021) is a cooked poultry dish consisting of the wing portion of the chicken, prepared with the skin removed and the bone retained, then gently braised in a seasoned liquid until tender. The braising process typically involves soy sauce, aromatics such as ginger and garlic, and sometimes a small amount of sugar or rice wine, yielding a soft, moist texture with a savoury umami flavour. Removing the skin significantly reduces the fat content compared to skin-on preparations. Per 100 g, this food provides 181 kcal, 27.18 g protein, 7.18 g fat, and 0.0 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate food suitable for low-carbohydrate and ketogenic dietary patterns. Sodium is low at 73 mg/100 g in the base preparation, though braising sauces can increase this substantially. Cholesterol is moderate at 74 mg/100 g. This dish is commonly consumed in Singapore and across Chinese-influenced cuisines as a home-cooked or hawker-style protein dish. It is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension when prepared with minimal added sodium, and is halal-certified when prepared with halal-compliant ingredients.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo Database (F11010021) at confidence 0.95, which is the primary approved regional source for Singapore food records. The food identity is exact and the source match is direct.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo Database (F11010021); this is a protein-dominant food with no starch or sugar in the base preparation — confirmed zero. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per the same source. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as chicken meat contains no dietary fibre. Saturated_fat_g estimated at approximately 2.0 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central reference for cooked skinless chicken wing (NDB 05065); HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown for this entry. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, water content, and full vitamin/mineral panel not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry; USDA FoodData Central (NDB 05065, chicken wing meat only, cooked, roasted) consulted as secondary reference but preparation method differs (roasted vs braised), so values not substituted to preserve source integrity. Glycemic index field is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value for a negligible-carbohydrate food (0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g); gi_category is set to not_applicable and gi_source documents that GI testing is not applicable for zero-carbohydrate protein foods. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0 — naturally zero, documented), sodium_mg (73.0), saturated_fat_g (2.0 — estimated from USDA secondary reference, documented in notes), sugar_g (0.0 — confirmed zero, documented). All four required secondary fields present as numeric values.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to numeric 0 (sentinel value for not-applicable negligible-carbohydrate food). gi_category set to not_applicable. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source present with full documentation explaining that GI is not applicable for this zero-carbohydrate protein food. Validator requirement for numeric glycemic_index satisfied.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn closest_parent match documented (FOODON_03302050, chicken wing food product). FoodEx2 closest_parent match documented (A0CKN, chicken wing cooked). SNOMED CT null with honest gap note. No codes invented.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid Arthritis included as additional condition.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'close' to 'closest_parent' and foodex2_match_type from 'close' to 'closest_parent'.

Iteration 3: Changed glycemic_index from null to numeric 0 to satisfy validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric. Updated gi_source notes to document that 0 is used as a sentinel value for not-applicable negligible-carbohydrate food. Updated notes field accordingly.

Nutrient data sourced from HPB Foodo F11010021 (approved primary source, confidence 0.95). All zero and -1 values documented in notes. Singaporean multilingual naming complete. All three required health conditions present with clinical guidance. Aliases consistent between flat array and structured object. Ontology anchors honest with closest_parent match documentation.
